title: Status date is very out-of-date
body:
### Issue Description

The `STATUS` column of this container: Not sure how I had a computer 292 years ago, nevermind an LLM. I wonder what kind of power infrastructure would have been needed then…

```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                            COMMAND     CREATED       STATUS                    PORTS                     NAMES
c89f660aab05  docker.io/ollama/ollama:latest                   serve       2 months ago  Exited (0) 292 years ago  0.0.0.0:11434->11434/tcp  ollama
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

No idea. Just observed this state.

### Describe the results you received

Time travel?

### Describe the results you expected

I last used the container about 2 months ago, but the container I used it in tandem with has a "2 months ago" status entry.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Just an observation so far.

comments.body: Unless there is a reproducer it is impossible to tell. It just records a timestamp when the container exited. If it just happened once it is easy to blame some disk corruption/bit flip.
As we don't know how to reproduce this I am going to close this, feel free to comment again here if you know how to trigger that
Not that I have any more concrete information, but DailyWTF has an instance of a related issue: https://thedailywtf.com/articles/dash-it-all
I just noticed it on another machine too. I wonder if it has to do with the machine shutting down and/or losing power while running a container?
